features.summary: Raytheon Technologies reported Q3 2022 results with 6% organic sales growth to $17 billion and adjusted EPS of $1.21, ahead of expectations. The company secured over $22 billion in awards during the quarter, driving backlog growth of $2 billion sequentially. Commercial aerospace recovery continued with aftermarket sales up 24% year-over-year, while defense businesses faced supply chain and labor constraints. Full-year sales guidance was reduced to $67-67.3 billion, but adjusted EPS guidance was raised at the bottom end to $4.70-4.80.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued commercial aerospace recovery with global air traffic reaching 90% of 2019 levels by year-end and at or above 2019 levels by end of 2023. Defense demand remains strong with robust order pipeline, though supply chain and labor constraints will persist into 2023. The company anticipates mid-to-high single-digit sales growth in 2023 with segment operating profit growth exceeding sales growth, enabling margin expansion despite ongoing inflationary pressures.
features.risks: Primary risks include persistent supply chain disruptions affecting 400 of 13,000 suppliers, labor availability constraints requiring 10,000 additional workers, and inflation headwinds of approximately $2 billion versus initial $1.5 billion estimate. Additional risks include potential $0.40 pension headwind in 2023, geopolitical uncertainties, and the impact of Section 174 R&D capitalization requirements creating cash flow timing issues.
features.key_drivers: Key growth drivers include $168 billion backlog providing multi-year revenue visibility, strong commercial aftermarket recovery with 24% growth, and elevated global defense spending driving demand for hypersonic systems, air defense, and F-35 programs. The company is investing $9 billion in R&D and capital expenditures while executing 500+ cost reduction projects and implementing pricing actions to offset inflation, positioning for significant growth as near-term headwinds recede.
